title: senna acutifolia
intro: Senna acutifolia, also known as Alexandrian senna, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family and is widely cultivated for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and pods contain anthraquinone glycosides, which have been used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including constipation and irritable bowel syndrome. Senna acutifolia has been traditionally used to promote bowel movements, relieve symptoms of indigestion, and support the treatment of hemorrhoids. Herbal preparations of Senna acutifolia include teas, capsules, and tablets, often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
